data.title: Trigon Metals receives $1M from Horizon, expects $2M by August 4th 2025, showing commitment after shareholder vote.
data.description: Trigon Metals Inc. has received an initial payment of US$1 million out of a total US$2 million advance from Horizon Corporation Ltd. The rest is expected to be paid by August 4, 2025. This accelerated payment comes after a successful Trigon shareholder vote on July 4, 2025. The company's stock symbols are TSX-V: TM, OTCPK: PNZTF, and FRA:TZU2.

data.title: Coinbase stock plunges as company misses Q2 revenue forecasts, generating $1.5B revenue, below expectations.
data.description: The cryptocurrency exchange headquartered in the United States generated $1.5 billion in revenue, falling short of what analysts had predicted.

data.title: Bitcoin miner Riot Platforms Inc (NASDAQ: RIOT) reported Q2 revenue of $152.99 million, beating estimates. Earnings per share: 57 cents, surpassing expectations for a loss of 10 cents per share.
data.description: Riot Platforms Inc, trading as NASDAQ: RIOT, disclosed its Q2 financial outcomes post-market on Thursday. In the second quarter, the company generated a revenue of $152.99 million, surpassing analyst predictions of $147.65 million. The Bitcoin mining firm also revealed earnings for Q2 at 57 cents per share, outperforming expectations of a loss of 10 cents per share as indicated by Benzinga Pro. Riot expressed that they had attained... The detailed article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Pharmacy Profiles partners with Delaware Pharmacy Society to verify credentials for pharmacy professionals in the state.
data.description: On July 31, 2025, Pharmacy Profiles revealed a recent collaboration with the Delaware Pharmacy Society to offer thorough credentials verification services for pharmacy professionals in Delaware. This partnership highlights a mutual dedication to ensuring the accuracy of professional qualifications within the state's pharmacy sector.

data.title: Sagen MI Canada Inc. reports $111 million in Q2 2025 net income, down $40 million from prior year; declares $0.3375 dividend.
data.description: Sagen MI Canada Inc. (TSX: MIC) disclosed a net income of $111 million for the second quarter of 2025; a decrease of $40 million from the same quarter last year. This drop was primarily attributed to a decline in net insurance service result and investment income. The Board of Directors also declared a dividend of $0.3375 per Class A preferred share, Series 1, scheduled for payment on September 29, 2025. Holders of record as of September 15, 2025, will be eligible for this dividend. The company confirms that all dividends are accounted for Canadian federal, provincial, or territorial income tax purposes. Full details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: $18.2B profit for Amazon due to strong retail demand, surpassing consumer spending expectations, with cloud computing margins tightening.
data.description: Shoppers surpassed projected spending on the online retail platform operated by the company, with an increase in sales, while profit margins decreased notably in the organization's critical cloud computing sector.

data.title: Roku beats revenue expectations in Q2, reports $1.11 billion revenue with 15% increase YoY. Platform revenue grows 18%, devices revenue falls by 6%. Earnings per share at 7 cents, surpassing estimates.
data.description: Roku Inc (NASDAQ: ROKU) released its second-quarter financial results after the market closed. The company reported revenue of $1.11 billion, a 15% increase from the previous year, surpassing the Street's expected $1.07 billion. Platform revenue saw significant growth, reaching $975.5 million, up 18% year-over-year, driven by successful video advertising and the acquisition of Frndly. Device revenue, however, decreased to $135.6 million, down 6% compared to the previous year. Earnings per share for the quarter were 7 cents, well above the expected loss of 16 cents per share.

data.title: Cloudflare's Q2 revenue exceeded expectations at $512.32 million, adjusted EPS at 21 cents. Stock rises post-results.
data.description: Cloudflare Inc (NYSE: NET) released its Q2 financial report, revealing a revenue of $512.32 million, exceeding the projected $501.33 million, with an adjusted EPS of 21 cents compared to the estimated 18 cents. This marks a 28% increase in total revenue year-over-year. The company delivered $99.8 million in operating cash flow and $33.3 million in free cash flow within the quarter, concluding with $3.96 billion in cash, cash equivalents, and available-for-sale securities. Cloudflare is focused on rapid innovation to drive its growth. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: MEG Energy reports successful Q2 2025 with major turnaround completion and wildfire response; announces 10% increase in quarterly dividend to $0.11 per share.
data.description: MEG Energy Corp. reported its Q2 2025 operational and financial results, highlighting completion of the largest planned turnaround in its history. The company maintained safety during wildfire challenges and doubled its site population with zero injuries. A 10% dividend increase was approved, with a quarterly cash dividend of $0.11 per share payable on October 15, 2025. The Phase 2B Turnaround was completed on time and budget, while the Facility Expansion Project (FEP) is on track for completion in 2027. Financially, MEG generated adjusted funds flow of $125 million and production of 63,502 bbls/d, with planned turnaround and wildfire delays affecting results. The company returned $35 million to shareholders and maintained its 2025 guidance. Operating and financial results for Q2 2025 showed improvements in bitumen production, sales, and costs, with revenues of $757 million.

data.title: Definity Financial Corp. reports 9.1% growth in Q2 2025 GWP to $1.34B, $98.9M operating net income. Acquisition of Travelers Canada for $3.3B.
data.description: Definity (TSX: DFY) reported a Gross Written Premium growth of 9.1% in Q2 2025. Their combined ratio was 92.9%, operating net income was $98.9 million, and book value per share was $31.39. They announced the acquisition of Travelers Canada for $3.3 billion and highlighted a strong financial position with solid growth in various lines of business. Gross written premiums for Q2 increased by 7.9%, with personal lines up 6.9% and commercial lines up 10.0%. Net underwriting revenue was up 10.5% in Q2, with a claims ratio of 63.2% and an expense ratio of 29.7%. Operating ROE was 9.6% for the twelve-month period ending June 30, 2025. Personal auto gross written premiums were at $564.4 million, property at $346.0 million, and total personal insurance at $910.4 million in Q2 2025. Commercial insurance gross written premiums were $427.0 million. Operating net income decreased from Q2 2024, mainly due to higher catastrophe losses. Overall, there was a positive financial performance and strategic growth trajectory for the company.

data.title: AKITA reports Q2 net income of $2.3M, debt repayment of $10M, and launch of normal course issuer bid on Aug 6, 2025.
data.description: AKITA Drilling Ltd. reported a net income of $2.3 million for Q2 2025, a significant improvement from a loss of $0.5 million in the same period last year. Adjusted funds flow from operations increased to $9.8 million in Q2 2025 from $6.4 million in Q2 2024. Net cash from operations rose to $18.2 million in Q2 2025 compared to $10.9 million in Q2 2024. The company's total debt reduced to $39.7 million from $52.4 million in Q2 2024, resulting in a debt to EBITDA ratio of 0.53:1. Total revenue for Q2 2025 was $49.6 million, up 29% from the previous year. Operating margin increased by 40% to $11.9 million. The company also announced the initiation of a return of capital strategy through a normal course issuer bid. Canadian drilling division revenue saw a total of $10.9 million in Q2 2025, up 11% from the previous year. Total assets increased by 6% to $258.0 million. The full news story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: ARC Resources Ltd. reported Q2 2025 results with 357,228 boe/day production and $396M net income. They acquired Kakwa Assets for $1.6B. Revised 2025 guidance includes $1.85B - $1.95B capex and expected production of 385,000 - 395,000 boe/day. ARC distributed $188M to shareholders in Q2.
data.description: ARC Resources Ltd. reported its second quarter 2025 financial and operational results. They achieved a production of 357,228 boe per day, with 61% natural gas and 39% crude oil and liquids. Production increased by 8% year-over-year. ARC generated $682 million in funds from operations, $699 million in cash flow from operating activities, and $396 million in net income. They distributed $188 million to shareholders in the second quarter. ARC acquired the Kakwa Assets for $1.6 billion and invested $496 million in capital expenditures during the quarter. The company plans to invest between $1.85 and $1.95 billion in capital expenditures for 2025 and expects average daily production between 385,000 and 395,000 boe per day. Their full financial results can be found on their website and SEDAR profile.

data.title: University of Phoenix emphasizes quick skills acquisition with new curriculum; enhancing job performance demanded by employers.
data.description: A national advertising campaign has been introduced by the University of Phoenix in Phoenix, highlighting its unique curriculum that provides career-ready skills to adult learners in just weeks. Companies are emphasizing skills to enhance job productivity and foster employee growth. The university places a significant emphasis on assisting career-driven adult learners, reflected in the Student End-of-Course Survey. This initiative aims to upskill individuals efficiently and cater to industry demands.

data.title: Eldorado Gold announces renewal of normal course issuer bid on July 31, 2025 in Vancouver, British Columbia after receiving approval from the Toronto Stock Exchange.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on July 31, 2025, Eldorado Gold Corporation disclosed that it has obtained approval from the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX) for its plan to renew the normal course issuer bid (NCIB). This bid allows the company to repurchase up to a specified amount of its outstanding common shares on the open market. The details of the repurchase program will soon be made public.

data.title: Legionnaires' disease lawyer probes Central Harlem outbreak: 22 sick, 1 dead. Investigation ongoing.
data.description: In Harlem, N.Y., on July 31, 2025, prominent Legionnaires' disease attorney Jory Lange is looking into a recent outbreak of the illness in Central Harlem. The outbreak has affected 22 individuals, resulting in the tragic death of at least one person. The cases of Legionnaires' disease are concentrated in...

data.title: Kevin Dillon of ERI discusses ITAD partnership best practices at 2025 ITAD Summit in Las Vegas with industry experts.
data.description: In Las Vegas, Kevin Dillon, Co-Founder and Chief Marketing Officer of ERI, joined a panel at the ITAD Summit held at the Bellagio Hotel & Casino. The event, featuring ITAD expert Karen Fedder, included discussions on various topics related to ITAD and data destruction, with participation from industry leaders like Miranda Monahan. ERI, known for being the first carbon neutral resource recovery and ITAD provider in the nation, showcased its expertise in sustainability and technology solutions at the summit.

data.title: Genpact appoints Thimaya Subaiya to their Board of Directors, effective immediately, bringing expertise in scaling services.
data.description: In New York on July 31, 2025, Genpact (NYSE: G), a technology services company, revealed the appointment of Thimaya Subaiya to their Board of Directors. This announcement signifies an immediate commencement of his duties. Thimaya's appointment brings valuable experience in expanding services and solutions to the Company.
